key_insight: The paper introduces a quantum physics-informed neural network (QPINN) that can solve nonlinear integro-differential and fractional PDEs by combining variational quantum circuits with physics constraints.
engineering_relevance: This offers a new quantum-enabled tool for modeling nonlocal, memory-dependent systems that arise in materials, transport, and complex dynamical processes.
method: Uses an affine feature map and variational quantum circuits to build trigonometric trial solutions.
Trains with physics-informed losses for nonlinear IDEs and FIPDEs.
Provides two variants: N-QPINN with numerical quadrature and A-QPINN with auxiliary variables to avoid quadrature.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing accurate solutions and better performance than classical physics-informed neural networks on nonlinear IDEs and FIPDEs.
watch_item: Quantum circuit training and quadrature-based nonlocal terms may be computationally expensive and sensitive to implementation details such as circuit depth, sampling noise, and quadrature accuracy.
paper_title: Quantum Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Solving Integro and Fractional PDEs
